THE COURT ORDERED THE WEDNESDAY'S ASBESTOS CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE CONTINUED TO THE FOLLOWING DAY SO THAT IT WILL BE HEARD ON THE SAME DAY AS BRAYTON'S CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE. THE 8/18/10 CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE IS CONTINUED TO 8/19/10. THE 10/20/10 CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE IS CONTINUED TO 10/21/10. THE 12/15/10 CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE IS CONTINUED TO 12/16/10. THE COURT CONFER WITH COUNSEL RE TIMELINES FOR DEMAND EXCHANGE. A HEARING IS SET FOR 5/13/10 AT 1:30P.M. IN DEPT. 206 FOR COMMENTS. RESPONSES ARE DUE BY 5/7/10 AND MAY BE FILED UNDER THE COMPLEX ASBESTOS LITIGATION, CASE #828684. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: D. LAW AND MOTION 302, MOTION BY DEFENDANT WARREN PUMPS, LLC TO TRANSFER VENUE FROM SAN FRANCISCO SUPERIOR COURT TO MARIN COUNTY SUPERIOR COURT, STATE OF CALIFORNIA. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE FROM SAN FRANCISCO SUPERIOR COURT TO MARIN COUNTY SUPERIOR COURT, STATE OF CALIFORNIA, IS DENIED. DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION RESIDES IN SAN FRANCISCO AND WAS NAMED AS A DEFENDANT BEFORE IT FILED FOR BANKRUPTCY. DEFENDANT IS A NEW JERSEY CORPORATION THAT HAS NOT DESIGNATED ANY PRINCIPAL PLACE OF BUSINESS IN CALIFORNIA, THEREFORE VENUE IS PROPER IN ANY COUNTY IN CALIFORNIA. A CORPORATE DEFENDANT CHALLENGING VENUE MAY OBTAIN AN ORDER CHANGING VENUE ONLY TO THE COUNTY OF ITS PRINCIPAL PLACE OF BUSINESS. SEE KARSON INDUSTRIES, INC. VS. SUPERIOR COURT (1969) 273 CAL.APP.2ND 7,8; EASTON VS. SUPERIOR COURT (1970) 12 CAL.APP.3RD 243. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR CONTINUANCE IS DENIED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) Notice Of Motion By Defendant Warren Pumps, Llc¿S Motion To Transfer Venue To Marin County; Memo Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Richard Ames; Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Jul-24-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Motion By Defendant Warren Pumps, LLC To Transfer Venue from San Francisco Superior Court to Marin County Superior Court, State of California. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to transfer venue from San Francisco Superior Court to Marin County Superior Court, State of California, is denied. Defendant Plant Insulation resides in San Francisco and was named as a defendant before it filed for bankruptcy. Defendant is a New Jersey Corporation that has not designated any principal place of business in California, therefore venue is proper in any county in California. A corporate defendant challenging venue may obtain an order changing venue only to the county of its principal place of business. See Karson Industries, Inc. vs. Superior Court (1969) 273 Cal.App.2nd 7,8; Easton vs. Superior Court (1970) 12 Cal.App.3rd 243. Plaintiff's request for continuance is denied. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
